column1: # Curated monetary-base / broad-money figures for the top fiat
# Refresh cadence: quarterly. Each central bank publishes an
# authoritative monthly or quarterly aggregate; the source column
# below identifies the exact series so an operator can refresh
# Methodology: we use M2 (broad money: currency + checking +
# savings + small time deposits) wherever the central bank
# publishes it under that name. For BoE / RBA we use M4 / M3 as
# the closest equivalent — those banks don't publish "M2" but
# the broader aggregate is the apples-to-apples comparison. For
# CNY M2 we follow PBoC's domestic M2.
# `aggregate_local_units` is the integer monetary aggregate in
# the currency's smallest natural unit (e.g. dollars for USD
# euros for EUR — NOT cents). `as_of` is the data-vintage date
# the central bank stamped on the series. `source` is a free-text
# series identifier callers can paste into the central bank's
# A row's absence yields NULL `circulating_supply` + NULL
# Coverage today: 106 of 110 currencies served by the Massive
# forex feed. Round 1 (lines 41-65) covers the developed-market
# majors (>95% of global fx spot volume per BIS Triennial 2022)
# from each central bank's preferred aggregate. Round 2 expands
# via the World Bank Indicator API (FM.LBL.BMNY.CN — broad money
# in current LCU). Round 3 hand-curates the WB-misses for
# economies the WB doesn't publish broad-money for.
# The 4 remaining uncovered tickers are intentional:
#   XPF (CFP franc) — pegged to EUR; broad money tracked but
#                     not as a clean LCU series
#   CNH (offshore CNY) — same currency as CNY
# Refresh the WB rows via `go run ./scripts/ops/circulation-fetch
# > /tmp/wb.csv`; refresh the central-bank rows by visiting each
# bank's stats portal (the source-column identifier is the search
# key). Annual cadence is sufficient — broad-money figures don't
# move enough month-to-month to be worth more frequent refresh.
# Lines starting with `#` are comments. Trailing whitespace OK.
# Generated by scripts/ops/circulation-fetch. Annual cadence; refresh
# yearly by re-running the script. WB's broad-money series uses
# slightly different methodology than each central bank's preferred
# aggregate but is the most consistent cross-country source.
# Round 3 (2026-05-08) — hand-curated for currencies WB doesn't
# publish FM.LBL.BMNY.CN for. Each row sourced from the named
# central bank's most-recent published M2/M3 figure.

column2: pegged to EUR; broad money tracked by IEOM

column1: #         but not as a clean LCU series
